name: Black Stars Rise
intro_text: Mont-Saint-Michel. The monastery rises from the sea like a dream. Or a nightmare.
The cult has gathered here for their final rite. Above, the black stars are aligning. Below, the tide is rising — and it is not water.
You must choose: seal the flood from below, or stop the vortex above. You cannot do both. The King in Yellow is nearly here.
setup_instructions: This scenario has two parallel agenda decks: Flood (left) and Vortex (right). Use the deck that matches your campaign's current path.
Investigators begin at Porte de l'Avancée.
All locations begin face-down. Reveal them as investigators explore.
Hastur (03291) is set aside. He enters play if both agendas reach their final card.

R1.label: Resolution 1 — The flood is sealed
text: You close the rift below Mont-Saint-Michel. The waters recede. The vortex above grows brighter — but it is incomplete without the flood.
You have bought the world one more night.
Record in the campaign log: *The flood was sealed. Continue to Dim Carcosa.*

R2.label: Resolution 2 — The vortex is closed
text: You shatter the ritual circle. The vortex collapses, pulling the cult's leader with it. But the flood continues to rise.
Record in the campaign log: *The vortex was closed. Continue to Dim Carcosa.*

defeat.label: Defeat — The stars align
text: Both the flood and the vortex complete. The stars are right. The King in Yellow steps through.
Record in the campaign log: *The investigators were defeated at Black Stars Rise. Campaign ends in defeat.*
